summary: Commodity production register from Incahuasi Sector C — the first analyzed khipu from this production/storage area (distinct from Sector A administration). 176 cords, AB-dominant (81% = highest single-color concentration at Incahuasi), 3 colors, 24 sections, 1-PAIRE with L4 depth. Only 3 STRING (all qaqa = section labels, 100% hits). Found in 'Big pile', matched to companion UR267-B. 18 L-1 cords (10% data loss).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  176 cords. 3 STRING (2%, all qaqa, 100% hits), 124 INT, 31 EMPTY (18%), 18 L-1 (10%).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L4. 24 clusters.
  3 colors: AB(143 = 81%), MB(32), KB(1).
  Provenance: Incahuasi — Sector C, Subsector 5, Unidad 19K, UE 2.
  Museum: Proyecto Incahuasi. OKR: 'Big pile', 'UR 267-B Match'.

SECTOR C vs SECTOR A
  First khipu from Sector C (production/storage). Sector A = administration (UR270/278/268/277/272).
  'Big pile' = mass storage of khipus. UR267-B = companion document.

AB DOMINANCE (81%)
  Highest single-color concentration among Incahuasi khipus. Suggests SINGLE-COMMODITY register.
  3-color system: AB = primary data, MB = section markers/subtotals, KB = single special cord.

24 SECTIONS
  24 clusters averaging 7.3 cords each. Possibly 2 years monthly tracking, or 24 production zones.
  1-PAIRE + L4 depth = hierarchical: total -> subcategory -> detail -> fine detail.

NARRATIVE
  Rock. Three times, and nothing more. That is all the text this khipu carries — the word *qaqa*, repeated three times across 176 cords of numbers. In the Incahuasi archive, *qaqa* is the universal label: it marks sections in UR270, UR268, UR277, UR278.
  This khipu was found in a pile — the OKR notes call it 'Big pile' — in Sector C of Incahuasi, far from the administrative buildings of Sector A. Sector C was production and storage. Matched to a companion called UR267-B.
  143 of 176 cords are light brown (AB) — 81%. Twenty-four sections, each a cluster of AB numbers with an MB marker at the boundary. The architecture descends four levels. This is a warehouse ledger, detailed enough to track individual batches.
  What commodity? The khipu does not say. But the location, the structure, and the companion match suggest one register among many in a storehouse where goods were counted, recorded, and tied in colored thread.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Incahuasi Sector C analysis
Cross-corpus: UR270 (peanut, Sector A), UR268 (district summary), UR277 (inventory)
OKR: 'Big pile', 'UR267-B Match', Sector C Subsector 5 UE 2
